entries.reasoning: Building on the best 3-coil QA parent but switching to MUSE surface with 4 coils and maximum allowed force/torque thresholds to address the severe violations. Using baseline MUSE thresholds for cc/cs.
Using the 4-coil parent with smallest force violations as base. Switching to MUSE with high but not maximum force/torque limits and relaxed engineering constraints to find feasible solutions.
Exploring 3 coils on MUSE with maximum force/torque limits and baseline engineering constraints to establish if fewer coils can achieve feasibility with the new surface geometry.
Building on the parent with very tight cc margins. Switching to MUSE with 4 coils instead of 3, maximum force/torque limits, and minimum allowed cc threshold to test feasibility.
Testing if 5 coils can distribute forces better and achieve feasibility with more moderate force/torque thresholds while maintaining reasonable engineering constraints.
Using the 4-coil parent with moderate force violations as base. Switching to MUSE with high force/torque limits and relaxed but reasonable engineering constraints.
Exploring maximum coil count to see if 6 coils can distribute forces well enough to achieve feasibility with more aggressive force/torque thresholds while using tighter engineering constraints.
Mutating a 3-coil parent to MUSE with 4 coils instead of 3, high force/torque limits, and relaxed engineering constraints to balance feasibility with performance on the new surface.

Testing if MUSE surface geometry naturally produces lower forces by using moderate force/torque thresholds with very relaxed engineering constraints to see if we can achieve both force feasibility and good coil quality.
Using a 3-coil parent as base, switching to MUSE with moderately high force/torque limits and near-baseline MUSE thresholds to test if MUSE geometry produces lower forces than QA.

Building on the best 5-coil QA parent but switching to QH surface with maximum allowed force threshold and relaxed engineering constraints to address the severe force violations seen in all QA runs.
Using the second-best 5-coil parent as base but reducing to 4 coils on QH surface with high force threshold and moderate engineering constraints, following the baseline QH configuration.
Starting with baseline QH reference case but with relaxed force threshold to establish feasibility on this under-explored surface before tightening constraints.
